Gift_Ideas: Concert tickets to an act he would actually like (check Ticketmaster presales). Galway International Arts Festival programme if anything good is on. A night away in Connemara, he has been asking about Ballynahinch.
Notes: He is hard to buy for because he says he doesn't want anything and means it. The Ballynahinch suggestion came from him. Put it on the list.

Gift_Ideas: Something for the kitchen he would use. A new dough scraper and proving basket set. Or a cooking class with a local baker if available.
Notes: He will not want something expensive. The sourdough kit would make him happy.

Gift_Ideas: Handmade card, send the week before. A quilting book she hasn't seen. Or a gift voucher for the Connemara fleece shop in Clifden if she would use it. Or just drive up and take her for lunch.
Notes: She genuinely prefers a phone call and the visit over anything wrapped. The lunch is the gift.

Gift_Ideas: Something for the B&B or for her personally. Lavender soap and hand cream set from the Saturday market. Or new quilting thread in colours she's mentioned.
Notes: Homemade or local always. Never anything generic.

Gift_Ideas: Drive to Clifden. Bring something from the Saturday market he would appreciate. Whiskey. Good cheese. Connemara black pudding if the stall is running.
Notes: He does not want to make a fuss. The visit is the gift. Bring the food and sit with him.

Gift_Ideas: Together with birthday drive. Already in Clifden.
Something for the kids to give him from them. Fiona and Oscar could decorate a card. Or a voucher for his favourite restaurant, Ard Bia or The House Hotel.
Notes: He is easier than Colin. A nice dinner voucher works.

Gift_Ideas: Something thoughtful. She likes pottery. The Saturday market ceramics stall has good pieces. Or a weekend away voucher for herself without the kids.
Notes: She does everything and never asks for anything. Something that is specifically for her.

Gift_Ideas: Move the Saturday coffee to Ard Bia instead of Kai. Bring a handmade card. Maybe a gift from the market: a candle, a piece of jewellery from the craft stall.
Notes: It's the time and the ritual that matters to her.

Gift_Ideas: Something for the house. A piece of pottery. A voucher for a Connemara weekend away. Or a contribution to their honeymoon fund if they set one up.
Notes: She is one of the most important people in my life. The gift should reflect that without being showy.

Gift_Ideas: Picture book. Art supplies. Something for a 5-year-old.
Notes: Check with Amy what she would actually like and doesn't already have.

Gift_Ideas: Something for a 3-year-old. Construction toys. Books.
